WordPress.Is.Dead
  • Home
  • Manifesto
  • vs. WordPress
  • Eulogy
  • The Proof
Exhibit A

The whole site.
One sentence.

Here is the verbatim prompt that produced every page on this domain, including this one. Including the eulogy. Including the navigation that brought you here. 

— The Prompt —
Generate a static html website with 3-5 unique pages all about how
WordPress is dead because Claude just generated this entire website
for me in one prompt.

That is it. That is the input. No design brief. No wireframes. No Figma. No "can you also write the copy." No back-and-forth. No "make it pop." Just a sentence with a hyphen in it.

5
Pages produced
1
CSS file, hand-written
0
Frameworks loaded
1
Sentence, total
What the model decided on its own

Things you did not ask for, but received anyway.

Because the prompt was a single sentence, every aesthetic and editorial decision below was inferred from tone alone. Receipts:

A

The brutalist red-and-black palette

Picked because the site is rude on purpose. Softer colors would have undercut the bit.

B

The serif eulogy

Switching to Georgia inside a frame, on a site that is otherwise sans-serif, sells the funeral metaphor without saying "funeral."

C

The hyphenated "Wordpress.Is.Dead" wordmark

Reads like a domain name, which is the joke. The red dots are the punchline.

D

Dates on the eulogy that match today

WordPress was released May 27, 2003. The site was generated April 29, 2026. The headstone got the math right.

E

Self-aware navigation

"Eulogy" and "The Proof" are not normal nav items. They are jokes that only work if you read all five pages, which is the actual goal of any website.

F

This card

You are reading a card, on a page, on a site, that explains the existence of the card you are reading. WordPress could not have done this in one prompt.

The whole site is 34kb.
wp-admin is bigger than this.

Back to the front page →
